lede: A look at December 2 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Stephen McGinn and Britney Spears, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Ghost Ship warehouse fire
events.desc: Thirty-six people die in a fire at a converted Oakland, California, warehouse serving as an artist collective.

events.title: Northern Ireland
events.desc: The United Kingdom devolves political power in Northern Ireland to the Northern Ireland Executive following the Good Friday Agreement.

events.title: Ukraine
events.desc: Canada and Poland become the first nations to recognize the independence of Ukraine from the Soviet Union.

events.title: Benazir Bhutto
events.desc: Benazir Bhutto is sworn in as Prime Minister of Pakistan, becoming the first woman to head the government of a Muslim-majority state.

events.title: Cuba
events.desc: In a nationally broadcast speech, Cuban leader Fidel Castro declares that he is a Marxist–Leninist and that Cuba will adopt Communism.

events.title: Korean War
events.desc: Korean War: The Battle of the Ch'ongch'on River ends with a decisive Chinese victory and UN forces are completely expelled from North Korea.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: During the Manhattan Project, a team led by Enrico Fermi initiates the first artificial self-sustaining nuclear chain reaction.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: Russia and the Central Powers sign an armistice at Brest-Litovsk, and peace talks leading to the Treaty of Brest-Litovsk begin.

events.title: Philippine–American War
events.desc: Philippine–American War: The Battle of Tirad Pass, known as the "Filipino Thermopylae", is fought.

events.title: Alabama
events.desc: Alabama ratifies the 13th Amendment to the U.S. Constitution, followed by North Carolina, then Georgia; U.S. slaves were legally free within two weeks.

deaths.text: Gerardus Mercator, Flemish mathematician, cartographer, and philosopher

observances.text: Christian feast day: Blessed Maria Angela Astorch
